title: panope stoechadifolia
parts: Panope stoechadifolia has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and flowers, which are used to treat various health conditions. The leaves of Panope stoechadifolia contain flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant are rich in iridoids, including stoechadifolin and panope, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The flowers of Panope stoechadifolia contain volatile oils, including pinene and limonene, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
